entry.title: Re: Development Goal Plan - Evergreen or Yearly?
entry.content:
Hi, I am in a the same situation.

We had the evergreen approach, and as we just upgraded to PMGM, accessing the Development Objectives from the Performance Form does not work well anymore.

Basically, as the user adds a new Dev Objective, it is defaulted to the Template start and end dates. Since these can't be blank, I have to allow a very wide date range (eg 1.1.2019 to 31.12.2027). Which then requires a lot of click for the user to select a relevant date.

Using yearly template make it not evident for the user to pick another template, or find development actions from prior year. As a significant amount of development goals span over more than one year, what is the ideal process ?

For now, the new PMGM looks better, but does not support our business process.

Any input welcome,

Best regards,

Kris

entry.title: Issue with SortByValue in SAC Table – Missing Data for Secondary Measure
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’ve encountered an issue in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) while working with a table in a story. The table is structured with:

  • Columns: Two measures
  • Rows: A single dimension

When I apply a SortByValue on one of the measures, the data corresponding to the second measure in the table does not display correctly (or seems to disappear).

This behavior is puzzling because it worked as expected before, but one day it stopped displaying correctly. I’m unsure if this is due to a recent SAC update or if the data is somehow “stuck” or misaligned.

Has anyone else encountered a similar issue? Could this be related to a configuration change, a potential bug, or limitations in handling multi-measure tables?

Any suggestions or advice on how to resolve this would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!

 

Before applying Sort by value:

 

After applying Sort by value:

entry.title: Currency Conversion Table - Category = Specific - "_" Underscore in Version Name
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have stumbled upon a curious error in Currency Conversion Table in SAC. I am using the following naming convention for Versions in planning models => "PLN_YYYY", f.e. "PLN_2025" which stands for PLAN_2025 public version. We have been using this naming convention for couple of years now and had no issues with it.

Today I tried to assign a currency conversion in the Currency Conversion Table to this specific Version but for some reason SAC does not accept the underscore "_" in the name of the Version (as you can see in the attached picture). Did this happen to any of you? Is this a new problem or has it been always like this? Is it possible to somehow make this work with the underscore or do we need to change the naming convention in order for it to work?

Thank you very much!

Adam

entry.title: SAP Personas - Admin question or rather your best practice
entry.content:
HI. 

I would like to know, how you are handling the admin of the flavors?

I am building flavors on sandbox (let's say client 210). I admin everything on 210, create transports and transfer them to QA and production. But as you know, often sandbox is erased for production data update, so my flavors and settings in 210 are ereased and production ones are copied (so if I don't backup, everything is lost 😞 )

I know, I should transfer everything to customizing client (let's say client 200) and that should be my original client for my flavors. But what is the best easy way?

 

How are you managing this approach? Beause if flavour is created in customizing client, no data is there for testing, that's why I use sandbox.

Do you create flavor in customizing client, create transport request and transfer it to the sandbox via SCC1? 

Or are you creating it in the sandbox and then sending it to the customizing client?

 

What is your best practice?

entry.title: Re: Select historical date range in a story
entry.content: Hi. Thanks for the response. I've figured it out. It was how to get that date selection so you could put individual dates in their. For anyone else wanting to know its:- Create your Input Control. Select your field (mine was Completed date). Select Filter by Range. Type is Fixed. Then select the maximum date range you might want to use in the Story. Then select Ok. Then adjust date range to desired range for the default. Done. Thanks

entry.title: Is it possible to increase the length of the WBS Description field in SAP S/4HANA Project System?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working with WBS, and I have encountered a limitation that I’m hoping the community can help me address.

The description field for WBS is currently restricted to 40 characters. Our projects often require more detailed descriptions to capture critical information about the tasks or activities represented by each WBS. Unfortunately, the 40-character limit makes this challenging, as we often need to truncate or abbreviate important details, leading to potential misunderstandings or lack of clarity. 

Please note that the filed should appear in Fiori and SAC. 

  • Is there any way to increase the character limit for the WBS description field in SAP S/4HANA?
  • Alternatively, are there best practices or workarounds that allow for storing more descriptive text for WBS elements?

Thanks for any help you may provide.

entry.title: Can we change the default back behavior for fiori elements app created using RAP?
entry.content:
Right now, the default behavior is if we click the back button that is within the app, it will prompt this dialog box to let us choose whether to discard or keep the draft. Can we disable this popup and by default use the Discard Draft option?

 

Currently there are 2 back buttons in the app which has different behavior. The back button in the browser just lets you go back to the main page and keeps the draft changes you have made. The other back button (beside the SAP logo) shows a dialog box to choose whether to Save, Keep or Discard the draft.

We are ok with the 'Keep' and 'Discard' option but the 'Save' option is kind of critical as it will update the data right away. And it might be kind of confusing for the user whenever they see popup as they think they are just saving the draft when in fact the system is actually updating the data.

entry.title: Re: Proposed Architecture for File Transfer into SAP Private Cloud
entry.content: @Naor_Shalomyour recommendation "For cost-sensitive or time-critical setups: Use Option 1 (Integration Suite via Cloud Connector)." may not always be wise. You are not considereing one essencial fact. SAP Integration Suite costs also take into to account the message size. So if file being transferred is bigger that 250Kb then it will count for 2 , or more messages, depending on its full size.

entry.title: DM POD Plugin API call issue
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to enhance the standard POD report by adding a custom column with the order status based on some calculations. This is achieved by calling different APIs in a hierarchy sequence.

I am setting values in a json model in LifecycleExtension.js file. These values are, among others, order & status.

Then in ExtensionUtilities.js file I collect these values and update the final report accordingly.

Problem is that in LifecycleExtension.js file I am calling several DM APIs.

Even though I am waiting for the asynchronous call to finish some calls have no response as they have not been finished...

So I have implemented Promise all, where I wrap all the different Promises ( all API calls: orders, boms, materials, etc. ). This is done in onAfterRendering: function inside LifecycleExtensions.js.  My assumption was that this Promise all would wait for all the API calls to be completed and then it would release the onAfterRendering: function and would jump to ExtensionUtilites.js where I resume the logic by reading what I have set up in onAfterRendering: function.   The problem is that it does not wait and it jumps to ExtensionUtilites.js even when Promise all is not completed

 

..however these calls are completed after some seconds but it is too late as ExtensionUtilities.js has been executed already.

I am forcing the API calls to be synchronous and that solves the issue but the screen freezes for a long time before it renders the results.

Does anybody have any solution?.

Thanks,

Javier

entry.title: New Videos on DMS with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I have published 2 short videos on the integration of SAP BTP Document Management Services with SAP Build Process Automation.

The first video explains how to connect DMS to SAP Build Process Automation, so that the File widget is available to add to forms.

The second video explains how to add the File widget to a form, how you can add an uploaded document to another form, how the document is represented by a text string (this is important when connecting to automations), and how the document is stored in the DMS repo.

These videos are follow-ups to the 3 Tech Byte videos on setting up DMS, and explaining the 3 forms of DMS: Integration, Application and Repository options.

entry.title: General Print Layout For 1099 Forms in US
entry.content:
In this blog post, I will inform you about the enhancements made to US 1099 Forms in the 2411 release. You can now use customized Crystal Reports (CR) layouts for non-system 1099 forms to be in compliance with any legislative modifications.

A 1099 Form is used to document payments received from someone who is not your employer, such as an individual, business, or government entity. Different types of 1099 forms report various types of income and how they were earned. They summarize the amount of income received by the company and the source of the payment.

Each 1099 form includes multiple boxes. There are several types of 1099 forms, and they are updated annually by the tax authorities. In Business One, our customers are required to provide the 1099 Forms to vendors who have received money from the company and are compliant with the legal requirements for 1099 Form reporting.

Currently, we have four 1099 Forms defined in the system available. You can find them in Administration -> Setup -> Financials -> 1099 Table. For each table, the boxes are defined according to the legal requirements. The user has the ability to define new 1099 Forms.

In the Vendor Master Data, it is possible to assign a specific 1099 Table and Box that is always applicable to that vendor. This selection can be modified on the A/P Invoice -> Accounting tab. After the A/P invoice is paid, this document will be included in the appropriate 1099 Form reporting. Go to Financials -> Financial Reports -> Accounting -> 1099/1096 Report. Choose Report, click the three dots button „1099 Form“, select only one 1099 Form, click the three dots button „1099 Boxes“ and run the report.

In the previous versions (2408 or earlier) there were two limitations.

  1. The print layout for user-defined 1099 Forms was not available. There was no document type defined in the Layout Designer for them.
  2. The PLD print layout for existing 1099 Forms did not include user-defined 1099 boxes. The values in these boxes are used as system variables in PLD, preventing users from customizing them themselves. The user-defined boxes are included in the „1099 Form Printing“, however, they are missing from the PLD print layout.

As of the 2411 release, a new document type has been added to the "Layout and Sequence" designer. The new Crystal Report (CR) has been assigned to this document type, named "1099 Form – Template – CR (System)". This allows users to preview and print the form for any user-defined 1099 Form. Users can customize this print form to meet the requirements of the Tax Authorities. Additionally, users can design any print form using the default CR layout.

I would like to give you tips on how to create a custom layout with a pre-printed form.

We are going to create a pre-printed print layout for the user-defined 1099 NEC Form. Our goal is to print the data of each vendor onto the specific fields of the following pre-printed form.

Prerequisite:

Create BOX table “1099 usr NEC”

With following box setup:

01 – Nonemployee compensation

04 – Federal income tax withheld

05 – State tax withheld

07 – State income

Our US Demo company data looks like this:

Print preview with default system CR:

Let’s do the editing:

Go to the pencil icon on the Layout Designer page and click on it. Then, select "1099 Form - Template - CR (System)" and click on the Edit button.

Save the report as 1099_usrNEC.rpt.

Locate the source XML on your machine in the %temp% folder and copy it to your work folder (note: this file only exists if you have previously previewed the system CR at least once).

The file can be found at .../AppData/Local/Temp/B1_CR_GenericForm.xml.

It contains the exact same data as our B1 form in the following structure: 1 Grid Header and 8 Grid lines, including 2 lines with totals.

If we examine the content of this source XML, we are interested in the value of this hidden field as it contains the information about the box code that we will need for our calculations later on.

To work with the preview feature, we need to establish a live connection to our company's database.

In the CR designer, navigate to Database -> Set Datasource location.

For XML, create a new ADO.NET(XML) connection and set the File Path to your B1_CR_GenericForm.xml.

For DB, create a new OLE DB (ADO) connection -> Microsoft OLE DB Provider for SQL Server and enter your DB server credentials and company name.

 

First, we need to filter the Total lines.

Go to Select Expert and set the following condition:

{B1_CR_GenericForm_FormGrid_uId6_line.column_uId1} <> ""

Next, we need to group our data based on the Vendor Code. To do this, go to the Group Expert and select the field "B1_CR_GenericForm_FormGrid_uid6_line.column_uId1" as the Group By field.

Expand Group Footer and insert picture of preprinted form.

You can drag and drop requested fields from the Field Explorer, for example.

So far, the print preview will appear like this:

Since the box code for each box is displayed on a different details line for a particular BP, we need to display this information on the group footer for this specific BP, so we need to create formulas.

F_01_evaluate:

WhilePrintingRecords;

shared stringvar F_01;

if {B1_CR_GenericForm_FormGrid_uId6_line.column_uId11} = "01"

then F_01 := {B1_CR_GenericForm_FormGrid_uId6_line.column_uId6};

This formula simply identifies on which details line is relevant box code amount.

We will need similar formulas for each box code value we want to display:

01 - Nonemployee compensation

04 - Federal income tax withheld

05 - State tax withheld

07 - State income

If we look at our saved system CR there are already preprepared formulas, we just need to double check if the box code name corresponds with our setup in company and make necessary adjustments if needed.

Evaluate formulas must be placed on details section simply because details section contains relevant data for evaluation.

However, we need to display this information later in our group footer with preprinted form, therefore each evaluate formula needs to have pair formula called F_01, F_04 … which is used for display only.

This formula displays calculated value thanks to shared variable from evaluate formula.

For example, F_01 will look like this

F_01:

WhilePrintingRecords;

shared stringvar F_01;

Now we can drag and drop prepared formulas to our Group Footer (align to right), for example:

So far, our print preview will look like this:

Since all the data appears to be correct, there is no need to display the details section anymore. To remove unnecessary sections, such as Page Header A, Page Header B, and Details ..., go to the Section Expert. Additionally, make any necessary adjustments such as changing the font size, aligning elements,...

The preview should currently look like the following:

Similarly, we can proceed with other related fields.

Finally, we change the title of the report to "1099 Form - usr NEC" by going to File -> Summary Info. Then, we save the CR report and import it back into the SAP Business One.

We can use the preview layout functionality by navigating to File -> Preview Layouts -> 1099 Form - usr NEC.

I believe that having the ability to customize the print layout will provide you with greater flexibility to meet the legislative requirements.

If you have any comments or suggestions, please inform us in the Comments section located below the Blog.

entry.title: #COE#DE01 Error Message: Cannot update or delete. Actuals have been posted.
entry.content:
Hi

We have recently switched on "external staffing" replacing scope item 1KC

We are now using a 3rd party resource management tool and using SAP API's to integrate into SAP. We have included and error logs from SAP at integration to notify the team if there are issues when integrating into SAP.

On the error log we are able to ascertain what some of the error messages are and know how to fix them, however there are two messages that we are encountering and have reviewed the audit trails and other media to ascertain what would the root cause be of the issue

They messages seem to be straight forward, however we want to ensure that we are not overlooking anything

1) Cannot update or delete. Actuals have been posted

2) Work package xxx Employee xxx is already staffed to resource demand 0015

When reviewing the audit trail, the error is not being created by the SAP API integrations and these seem to have been there from before

I've reviewed SAP Note 

2901254 - Error "cannot update or delete. Actuals have been posted" occurred when changing the duration of the work package.

This does not seem to have any link to the issue at hand

Example (Time and Material project)

Work Package duration 

The requested resource and the staffed resource in this instance is the same person

Planned: 1 hour in Jan 23

Staffed; 1 hour in Feb 24

Actual Time Posted 21 hours in Feb 24

We have also encountered issues where the resource has only been staffed, but not requested with the same error message

Your assistance in the matter will be appreciated

Thanks

Theresa

entry.title: Aveniq überzeugt als Pilotpartner für neue Zertifizierung 'SAP security operations'
entry.content:
Wir schätzen die langjährige Partnerschaft mit Aveniq, einer seit über zehn Jahren verlässlichen SAP-zertifizierten Partnerin, die inzwischen Zertifizierungen für vier ihrer Services vorweisen kann. In unseren Gesprächen betonte Aveniq, dass ihr Schwerpunkt im Jahr 2024 auf dem Thema Sicherheit liegen wird. Umso mehr freuen wir uns, Aveniq als Pilotpartnerin für die Einführung unserer neuen Zertifizierung SAP security operations gewonnen zu haben.

Vor der Einführung einer neuen Zertifizierung wählt SAP gezielt Partner aus, die den Zertifizierungsprozess in einer Pilotphase durchlaufen. So stellt SAP sicher, dass der Ablauf reibungslos funktioniert und der Inhalt optimal auf den jeweiligen Themenbereich abgestimmt ist. Mit ihrer umfassenden Expertise im Bereich SAP Security erwies sich Aveniq als optimale Partnerin für die Pilotierung der neuen Zertifizierung SAP security operations.

In folgendem Interview mit Aveniq erfahren wir, welche Services das Unternehmen im Bereich SAP Security für SAP Cloud Kunden anbietet. Zudem führen sie aus, warum sie Security als strategisches Schwerpunktthema für sich definiert haben und welche Bedeutung dieses Thema auch für ihre Kunden hat. Zudem beleuchten sie, warum sich Aveniq dazu entschieden hat, als Pilotpartnerin für die neue Zertifizierung SAP Security Operations mitzuwirken, und welche Vorteile sie sich von dieser Zertifizierung versprechen.

 

Vielleicht möchten Sie uns zunächst etwas über Ihr Unternehmen erzählen und welche Dienstleistungen Aveniq im Bereich Security für SAP Cloud Kunden anbietet?

Aveniq ist eine führende Schweizer IT-Dienstleisterin und primär in den Branchen Finanzdienstleister, Gesundheitswesen, Industrie, Versorger, öffentlicher Sektor und Bildungswesen tätig. Aveniq bietet ihren Kunden innovative Dienstleistungen für den modernen Arbeitsplatz, Hybrid/Cloud-Lösungen, SAP-Beratung und -Implementierung, Hyperautomation-Möglichkeiten, Digitalisierungslösungen sowie CAD/PLM-Lösungen und Beratungsdienstleistungen mit Schwerpunkt IT-Infrastruktur, IT-Security und Business Continuity. An den Standorten Baden, Bern, Dübendorf und Oftringen sind rund 650 Mitarbeitende tätig.

Aveniq bietet SAP Cloud Kunden eine Vielzahl von Dienstleistungen im Bereich Security:

  • Identity & Access Management: Verwaltung von Benutzer*innen, Rollen, Katalogen, SSO, Provisionierung, Zugriffskontrolle sowie Identitäts- und Zugriffs-Governance.
  • SAP Security Check & Monitoring: Überprüfung und Überwachung von SAP-Systemen.
  • SAP Cloud Connector Security: Gewährleistung der Sicherheit des SAP Cloud Connectors.
  • Unterstützung bei internen und externen Audits: Hilfe bei der Vorbereitung und Durchführung von Sicherheitsaudits.
  • Security Awareness & Trainings: Schulungen und Sensibilisierung für Sicherheitsthemen.
  • Code-Schwachstellenanalyse für SAP-Anwendungen: Analyse von Sicherheitslücken im Code von SAP-Anwendungen.
  • Reduzierung der Angriffsfläche: Massnahmen zur Verringerung der Angriffsfläche auf Applikations- und Systemebene.
  • Datenschutz und -sicherheit: Schutz und Sicherstellung der Privatsphäre und Datenintegrität.
  • EWA Security Findings: Behebung von Sicherheitsbefunden aus dem EarlyWatch Alert.
  • Beratungsdienstleistungen: Unterstützung bei der Implementierung und Optimierung von SAP-Sicherheitsstrategien, von der Analyse der Sicherheitsanforderungen über die Entwicklung von Sicherheitskonzepten bis hin zur Unterstützung bei der Umsetzung.

 

Was war ausschlaggebend für Aveniq, Security als Fokus-Thema zu setzen? Und warum ist das Thema so bedeutsam für Aveniq und ihre Kunden? 

Die Bedeutung der Informationssicherheit für den langfristigen Erfolg und für die Nachhaltigkeit in einer sich ständig verändernden, digitalen und vernetzten Geschäftswelt, ist massiv gestiegen. Angesichts der zunehmenden Cyber-Angriffe ist eine proaktive, strategische Herangehensweise an Informationssicherheit und Datenschutz unerlässlich. Aveniq hat eine durchgängige Security-Strategie entwickelt, die sich am Marktbedarf und an modernen Prinzipien orientiert und sowohl physische als auch digitale Sicherheit, Management-Sicherheit und betriebliche Sicherheit umfasst.

Das Thema Security ist besonders bedeutsam, weil es zentrale Vorteile bietet:

  • Ganzheitlicher Ansatz: Aveniq legt grossen Wert darauf, keine Unterscheidung zwischen IT und OT zu machen und einen umfassenden Ansatz zu verfolgen, der Security, Privacy und Protection gemäss dem Prinzip «Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability» nach NIST CSF 2.0 und ZeroTrust über alle Ebenen hinweg implementiert. Dies umfasst Systeme, Prozesse, Produkte, Informationen/Daten, Personen und Lieferanten, also den Schutz der Unternehmenswerte.
  • Verbesserte Sicherheit: Durch umfassende, präventive Sicherheitsmassnahmen wird ein hoher Schutz gewährleistet, der alle Angriffsvektoren abdeckt. Dies wird durch kontinuierliches Monitoring und schnelle Reaktionsmassnahmen (Response, Recover) unterstützt, die mit unserem Security Operations Center (SOC) sichergestellt sind.
  • Vertrauensbildung: Die Einhaltung internationaler Sicherheitsstandards und Datenschutzprinzipien, belegt durch verschiedene Zertifizierungen wie ISO 27001, ISO 22301 und weitere, stärkt das Vertrauen.
  • Anpassungsfähigkeit: Flexibilität und Kontrolle im hybriden und Multi-Cloud-Modell ermöglichen es, spezifische Kundenbedürfnisse effizient zu erfüllen.
  • Effizienzsteigerung: Die Konsolidierung von Technologien und Prozessen führt zu einer verbesserten Infrastruktur- und Anwendungsverwaltung und optimierten Kosten für die Kunden.
  • Kundenzentrierte Lösungen: Modular aufgebaute und replizierbare Services sind auf die individuellen Anforderungen und Geschäftsziele der Kunden abgestimmt und berücksichtigen dabei auch gesetzliche Anforderungen und Risikobereitschaft.

Diese Security-Fokussierung trägt zur Stärkung der Wettbewerbsposition der Kunden bei und ermöglicht es Aveniq, ihre Marktposition weiter auszubauen.

 

Warum hat sich Aveniq dafür entschieden, Pilotpartnerin für die neue Zertifizierung SAP security operations zu werden?
Aveniq hat sich seit der erstmaligen Zertifizierung als SAP Operations Partner im Jahr 2011 kontinuierlich weiterentwickelt und sich agil an die sich wandelnden Kundenbedürfnisse sowie an die strategische Ausrichtung von SAP angepasst. Mit einem breiten Spektrum an Dienstleistungen - von SAP HANA Operations-Services über verschiedene Betriebsmodelle wie RISE with SAP Services, über SAP Remote-Support bis zu SAP Hosting in der Aveniq Cloud sowie über Hyperscaler - durften wir uns mit unseren rund 100 internen SAP-Spezialist*innen im Schweizer Markt etablieren.

Als einer der ersten Partner der Schweiz stellten wir 2018 unsere Kompetenzen für SAP S/4HANA Solutions Operations mit einer Zertifizierung unter Beweis. Unsere Zertifizierung für SAP BTP operations im Jahr 2024 verdeutlicht die fortlaufende Innovation und unser Engagement, den Kunden fortschrittliche und sichere SAP-Lösungen zu bieten. Das umfassende Wissen im Bereich SAP Security ermöglicht es uns, auch einen entscheidenden Beitrag zur Sicherheit und Effizienz der SAP-Systeme unserer Kunden zu leisten. Daher war die Zertifizierung für SAP Security Operations der nächste logische Schritt, um unser Expertenwissen auch in diesem Bereich auf den Prüfstand zu stellen.
Dass SAP uns als Pilotpartnerin für die neue Zertifizierung ausgewählt hat, erfüllt uns mit Stolz und Dankbarkeit. Das gemeinsame Streben nach Exzellenz ist der Grundstein unserer langjährigen Partnerschaft auf Augenhöhe. Wir freuen uns sehr, dass die Qualität unserer Dienstleistung im Bereich SAP Security die Auditoren überzeugt hat.

 

Welchen Nutzen versprechen Sie sich von einer SAP-Zertifizierung im Bereich Security Operations für SAP-Cloud-Lösungen?

Wir sehen in einer SAP-Zertifizierung im Bereich Security Operations für SAP-Cloud-Lösungen mehrere Vorteile:

  • Die Zertifizierung stärkt das Vertrauen der Kunden in die Sicherheitskompetenz von Aveniq und unterstreicht die Glaubwürdigkeit des Unternehmens als vertrauenswürdige Partnerin für SAP-Sicherheitslösungen.
  • Die Zertifizierung stellt sicher, dass die Sicherheitsoperationen von Aveniq den höchsten Standards entsprechen und kontinuierlich verbessert werden.
  • Durch die Zertifizierung kann Aveniq ihren Kunden einen höheren Schutz und eine bessere Servicequalität bieten, was zu einer höheren Kundenzufriedenheit führt.
  • Die Zertifizierung festigt die langjährige Partnerschaft zwischen SAP und Aveniq. Eine Verbindung, von der beide Seiten profitieren.

Zusammenfassend lässt sich sagen, dass eine SAP-Zertifizierung nicht nur das Vertrauen und die Glaubwürdigkeit eines Unternehmens stärkt, sondern auch zur Optimierung von Prozessen und zur Erschliessung neuer Märkte beiträgt. Sie bietet einen klaren Nachweis für die Einhaltung von Standards und Qualitätsanforderungen; was sowohl für bestehende, als auch für potenzielle Kunden sowie auch für den Hersteller selbst von grossem Wert ist.

entry.title: Easily perform a mass reset of expired or unused initial user passwords SU10
entry.content:
Hi colleagues,

To easily and quickly reset an expired or unused initial password, we need to follow these steps:

1.Use the SAP report RSUSR200, which we will execute from transaction SE38,or execute the SU10 transaction and select the " logon data" button.

 

Execute the SU10 transaction and select the logon data button :

 

 

Or Report RSUSR200 from se38:

In this report, filter by the following fields to generate a list of users who are experiencing issues with their initial password:

Select filters:

Execute : 

List of Users:

 

We export the usernames from the User column to a spreadsheet.

2. Execute the SU10 transaction and select the "Address  Data"  button:

 

Select the 'User' field and in the Multiple selection for S_BANAME, choose upload from the clipboard and copy the list of users from the spreadsheet.

 

Copy and execute 

 

1.Select All

2.Click Transfer button

 

On this screen, choose 'Select All' once more:

 

 

Click Change password 

 

Click Generate button

A new initial password will be created for the selected users. After execution, a list will appear with the text message: Generate password for user XXXXXX

 

I hope this can be helpful to someone. 😉

Thanks in advance

Best Regards!

entry.title: Warehouse Requests in SAP S/4 Hana Embedded EWM
entry.content:
Hi All 

I am writing blogs about warehouse request in sap s/4 hana embedded EWM.

What is Warehouse Requests ?

The warehouse request is a document  that enables the processing of warehouse activities for a specific product. Warehouse requests are planned documents for warehouse stock movements within and outside the warehouse. They form the basis for creation of warehouse tasks, which are assigned to warehouse workers for stock movement completion. Some of the warehouse requests that can be created in embedded EWM are outbound delivery orders, inbound delivery orders, posting change documents, and so on. In the following sections, we explain the business context in which the physical inventory process can be used in real-world scenarios and how to carry these processes out in the system.

SAP S/4HANA delivery or posting change requests become warehouse requests in embedded EWM. A warehouse request is the planning document for all internal movements in embedded EWM and is used only in embedded EWM. A warehouse request is made up of a document header and document type. The document header contains general data relevant to the entire request and the entire document; the document item contains data specific to each item in the delivery document, with the option to create follow-up documents and take follow-up actions for each item, independent of the delivery document header

The Warehouse Activities for a Product are as below

  • Picking
  • Putaway
  • Posting change
  • Stock transfer (within warehouse)
  • Scrapping

Type of warehouse requests

  • Inbound delivery
  • Outbound delivery order
  • Posting change
  • Internal stock transfer

Configuration

Service profiles are used in embedded EWM to define how documents are processed and to define the business features of the documents. These profiles are associated with the document type and item type in embedded EWM and control their features and behavior.

.There are two types of service profiles in embedded EWM.

 

System profile

System profiles are included in the scope of the standard embedded EWM system and can’t be modified. Internally, system profiles are linked to both document category profiles related to header settings and to item categories for item profile settings.

 

Customer profile

Customer profiles are required to manage individual business processes and can be modified based on business requirements. SAP has provided a set of predefined customer profiles, each of which is linked to a corresponding system profile. The customer profiles are assigned to document types and item types based on the specific business process for which they are used.

The various service profiles available in embedded EWM are explained in the following  sections

 

Process Code Profile

Process codes are used in warehouse requests to perform quantity adjustments at the item level. A process code profile determines which process codes are available for use in a warehouse request and is assigned at the item type level. SAP provides predefined process code profiles that are assigned to the corresponding system profiles. If you want to use different process codes for a business process, you can create a new process code profile and assign it to the system profile.

You can also create new process codes for a business process

IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Process Codes • Define Process Codes

 

 

To create a new process code profile or make changes to an existing one, navigate to IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Process Codes • Maintain Process Code Profiles

 

 

 

Status Profile

A status profile is used to control the status of a warehouse request to allow or prohibit processing from certain business transactions. You can use status management to set a status in a document for information purposes only or to change a document status after transaction execution.

 

To define a status profile, navigate to IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Process Codes • Define Status Profile

 

 

 

Quantity Offsetting Profile

Define the quantity offsetting profile  IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery Warehouse Requests • Quantity Offsetting • Define Quantity Offsetting Profiles

 

 

 

Text Profile

Use text types to copy text in the SAP S/4HANA delivery to the warehouse request in embedded EWM. You can also use text types to add text directly to the embedded EWM delivery. A text profile is used to group together text types for a delivery header or item. You can create text types for the delivery header and delivery item levels. Text types at the delivery header level include shipping instructions, bills of lading, and so on.

 

To define the text profiles for delivery documents via IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Text Management • Define Text Profiles with Access Sequence

 

 

 

Field Control Profile

A field control is used to determine which fields are displayed as display-only or modifiable fields during delivery processing. A field control profile contains all allowed fields and their attributes for a document or item type.

define a field control profile via IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery Warehouse Requests • Field Control • Define Field Control Profiles

 

 

 

 

 

 

Incompleteness Check Profile

Embedded EWM allows the system to perform an incompleteness check on a docu[1]ment to ensure that all required fields are filled before a document can be processed. These mandatory field names are mapped to a logical field name and contained in an incompleteness profile, which is assigned to a delivery document type for header fields and a delivery item type for item-related fields.

Defined IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Incompleteness Check • Define Incompleteness Profile

 

 

 

Action Profile

Embedded EWM uses the PPF for message/output control in delivery processing. All actions in a warehouse request document are generated by the framework based on action definitions and processed by the system. These actions can be print commands, email alerts, and so on. The actions are stored in an action profile assigned to a document type in EWM.

 

Set a PPF action profile via IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Actions • Change Actions and Conditions • Define Action Profiles and Actions.

 

 

 

Partner Profile

A delivery document in embedded EWM contains partner information required for further processing of the warehouse request. For example, the ship-to party is a man[1]datory business partner required in an outbound delivery order to determine the ship-to address for the delivery items. Partner processing provides the ability to maintain partner information at the delivery header or item level.

Define partner roles IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Partner Processing • Define Partner Roles

 

 

 

To create or change customer partner profiles IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Partner Processing • Define Partner Profiles

 

 

Reference Document Profile

 

A reference document profile allows you to add one or more preceding document references in embedded EWM. Each document is categorized using a reference document category code.

 IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Reference Documents • Define Reference Document Categories

 

Once the reference document categories are defined, they can be assigned to a reference document profile

IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Reference Documents • Define Reference Document Types Profiles

 

 

Date Profile

In embedded EWM, it’s possible to add new date types in warehouse request delivery documents to capture the date and time of various system processes for the document. SAP has provided predefined date types, but you can add new date/time types.

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Date/Times • Define Date Types

 

 

The new date/time types are defined, they can be bundled into date profiles

 IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Reference Documents • Define Date Profile

 

 

Process Profile

Embedded EWM uses process profiles for delivery headers and items, which control the processes allowed in a delivery document. Embedded EWM supports separate process profiles for delivery headers and items.

To define a process profile for a delivery header via IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery Warehouse Requests • Process Profile • Define Process Profile for Delivery Header

 

 

 

You can create process profiles for a document item

 IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Process Profile • Define Process Profile for Delivery Item

 

To create a number range for warehouse requests, navigate to IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Number Ranges

 

Using Inactive and Active Document Versions

EWM can accept or reject change messages for inbound deliveries from your enterprise management system, for example SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP. EWM uses inactive and active document versions for this. If warehouse activities (follow-on activities) have already begun for inbound deliveries, EWM rejects change messages from the enterprise management system. For example, EWM has already created a warehouse task (WT) for an inbound delivery. In doing so, EWM has also already set the status type Warehouse Activities to the status value Partially Completed. In the case of outbound deliveries, EWM can only accept a reduction of the quantity from the enterprise management system. If warehouse activities (follow-on activities) have already begun for inbound deliveries, EWM rejects the message from the enterprise management system.

Prerequisites

The document type of your request document contains an action profile with an action for activating the request document, for example /SCDL/IDR or /SCDL/ODR.

Note - If you do not want to activate your request documents immediately, either create your own action profile or do not assign an action profile to your request document type.

 

Please provide your feed back to improve it 

Reference https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/31d75d84edc2489b8124b0afaf3c374d.html

entry.title: Re: Pass select-options by reference
entry.content:
Hi,

the types being referenced must be identical to assign a reference (or castable, not relevant here).
But the table type being defined implicitly by SELECT-OPTIONS is simply not the same as period_range, even if they are structurally identical.

Look at this example: 

    TYPES: tt_one   TYPE RANGE OF d,
           tt_two   TYPE RANGE OF d,
           tr_one   TYPE REF TO tt_one,
           tr_two   TYPE REF TO tt_two,
           tr_three TYPE REF TO tt_two.

    DATA: a TYPE tr_one,
          b TYPE tr_two,
          c TYPE tr_three.

    a = b.  " The type of "B" cannot be converted to the type of "A"     
    b = c.  " fine

If you really need a reference (probably to manipulate the contents?), i would pass the select-options as changing parameter (preferably to the constructor) and get the REF inside.

BR,
holm

entry.title: Re: Display custom logic from abap program into cds view
entry.content:
Hi @MBartsch71, I have taken your suggestion but not sure where I am going wrong:
I have put my abap program code in a custom class as follows:

CLASS z_test_class DEFINITION

PUBLIC

FINAL

CREATE PUBLIC .

PUBLIC SECTION.

INTERFACES: if_rap_query_provider.

PRIVATE SECTION.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS z_test_class IMPLEMENTATION.

METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.

DATA: lt_swwwihead TYPE TABLE OF swwwihead,
      ls_swwwihead TYPE swwwihead,
      lv_days_outstanding TYPE i,
      lv_current_date TYPE d.

DATA(lo_paging) = io_request->get_paging( ).

 

" Handle paging "

DATA(lv_page_size) = lo_paging->get_page_size( ).

DATA(lv_max_rows) = COND #( WHEN lv_page_size = if_rap_query_paging=>page_size_unlimited THEN 0

ELSE lv_page_size ).

* Get the current date
lv_current_date = sy-datum.

* Select data from SWWWIHEAD table
SELECT * FROM swwwihead INTO TABLE lt_swwwihead WHERE wi_stat = 'READY'.

* Loop through the selected work items
LOOP AT lt_swwwihead INTO ls_swwwihead.
  * Calculate the number of days outstanding
  lv_days_outstanding = lv_current_date - ls_swwwihead-crdate.

  * Print the work item ID and the number of days outstanding
  WRITE: / 'Work Item ID:', ls_swwwihead-wi_id,
           'Days Outstanding:', lv_days_outstanding.
ENDLOOP.

ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

I have also added the annotaion "@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:Z_TEST_CLASS'" to my cds view:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZV_OUTSTANDING_ITEMS'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'Outstanding Work Items with Approvers'
@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:Z_TEST_CLASS'
define view ZV_OutstandingItems as select from swwwihead
  LEFT OUTER join swwuserwi on swwwihead.wi_id = swwuserwi.wi_id
{
  key swwwihead.wi_id,
  swwwihead.crdate,
  swwwihead.wi_text,
}

and have created a custom entity definition:

@EndUserText.label: 'definition view'

@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:Z_TEST_CLASS'

define custom entity Z_SCA_CUSTOM_REPORT_DEFINITION

{

key workitemid: abap.int8;

nextapprover: abap.char( 12 );

}

entry.title: SAP ECC Pricing Condition with Changing MVGR3 Value
entry.content: For sales quotes and orders, we have a pricing condition that uses values in VBAP-MVGR3.  We also have VC (variant configurator) and a value for MVGR3 (material group 3) is returned from the configuration and passed to pricing structure using routines ZXCEI0U01 called from EXIT_SAPLCEI0_001 and ZXVVAU10 called from EXIT_SAPFV45S_002.  Additionally, we then have some logic in MV45AFZZ USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE to override the MVGR3 value that comes from VC but only in certain situations.  The issue is we need to have pricing refire after the logic in USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE if the value of MVGR3 changes because the pricing condition may have a different value then.  Is there any way to accomplish this?  Or is there a different approach that should be taken?  I could move the override logic earlier, but my issue is the logic is based on VC config values which are not available at the earlier time that would accommodate the initial pricing.

entry.title: Variable Pay - Assistance Needed with Individual Goals Payout in Assignment-Based Ratings
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

I am experiencing issues with the system behavior for Individual Goals Payout while using Assignment-Based Ratings (ABR) and routing forms to the next step.

Here is my configuration:

  1. ABR for Individual Section coming from the PM form: only for completed forms, by summary, overall Objective:

2. In XML I have:

<assignment-based-rating enabled="true" force100ForEntryIndivPercent="true" force100ForEntryTeamPercent="true" oneAssignmentPerRow="false" bonusCurrencyOnAssignment="false">
<abr-team type="byForm" auto-update="false"/>
<abr-ind type="byForm" auto-update="false"/>
</assignment-based-rating>

3. On Design Worksheet I have totalIndividualCalcAmount (Ind Calc Amount), goalResultsPayoutAmountTotal  (Amt).

My objective is: allow certain users to adjust the Individual Section Payout based on guidelines.

Results:

When the individualPercent is updated at the worksheet level, the system recalculates the payout correctly. I can save the changes, and upon reopening the worksheet in the Executive Review, the values remain correct.

However, when the worksheet progresses to the next step, the individual payout reverts to its original value. I’ve already set the guideline setting "Force Default On Rating Change" to NO, but this has not resolved the issue.

I have 2 questions related to the above:

  1. Are there any additional settings or configurations that ensure 'refreshing' results in the ABR program that I could switch off? Alternatively, is using individualPercent unsupported in such programs?

  2. If individualPercent is supported in ABR programs, is there a specific way to configure the 'Force Comment' rule? I noticed it works for performancePercent, but is there any workaround or additional configuration in XML that could extend this functionality?

I look forward to your guidance and support.

Kind regards,

Joanna

entry.title: Re: Adding Variables to Predictive Analysis
entry.content:
Hi Ganjos,

You must provide future values for all the influencer, for all schools and over the whole forecast horizon.

What do I call past and future? Past and future is defined by the last available data point for the target. Referring to the example below, the last available value for BikeHires (the measure we want to predict) is on August 25, 2015. This implies that any later date is "the future". The predictions will start on  August 26, 2015.

This means that if you want to forecast using Temp. Mean and Rain as influencer, you must provide values for Temp. Mean and Rain starting from August 26, 2015 tup to the date of the last prediction.

In the example above it would be possible to get 6 forecast periods (August 26 to August 31) for the City Center area using Temp. Max and Rain as influencers. For the North area, it would be possible to get only 2 forecast periods as the Temp. Mean and Rain are available only for the next 2 days. 

Note that by default, forecast in table tries to predict as many point as allowed by the number of past data points. As you must provide future values for the whole forecast horizon,  you may want to reduce the number of forecast period using the Forecast Periods option in the advanced options:

Best,

entry.title: SAP HR Integration using MuleSoft
entry.content:
SAP HR systems is the main source of data for Employees starting from their recruitment till they are Terminated/retired from the company and even beyond that.

So, we would need HR data across various platforms – SAP & Non-SAP for various uses. These integrations help organizations achieve a seamless HR ecosystem, improve data accuracy, and enhance overall efficiency.

Some of the common examples are outlined below:

  • Payroll Systems (Eg: ADP/Workbrain)
  • Talent Management Systems (Eg: Workday/Cornerstone
  • Learning Management Systems (Eg; - Moodle/Talent LMS)
  • Benefits Administration Systems (Eg: bswift/BenefitFocus)
  • Time and Attendance Systems (Eg: Kronos/ADP WorkForce)
  • Employee Self-Service Portals (Eg: Service Now)
  • Third-Party HR Analytics Tools (Eg: Visier/Workday Analytics)

In this blog, I will talk about the cloud-based integration technique using MuleSoft which can be used for integrations from SAP HR. We can have a Remote Function call designed using ABAP and the same can be re-used to generate various interface files as per the vendor requirement.

Creating reusable RFC (Remote Function Call) function modules in SAP has several benefits, especially when integrating with platforms like MuleSoft. Below are some of the key advantages:

  • Reduced Development Time
  • Consistency
  • Simplified Maintenance
  • Easier Testing
  • Flexible Integrations
  • Quick Deployment
  • Lower Development Costs
  • Reduced Training Costs
  • Optimized Code
  • Reduced Redundancy
  • Comprehensive Documentation
  • Standardized Interfaces
  • Centralized Control
  • Compliance and Security

MuleSoft is a powerful platform for integration and API management, enabling organizations to connect applications, data, and devices. It's particularly useful for creating seamless integrations with SAP ECC HR systems.

Below are the detailed steps to set up MuleSoft

Step 1: Set Up Your Environment

  • Install MuleSoft: Download and install MuleSoft Anypoint Platform.
  • Install SAP Java Connector (JCo): Ensure you have the SAP Java Connector libraries installed, as MuleSoft uses JCo for RFC communication.

Step 2: Create RFC Destination in SAP

  • Open SAP ECC: Use transaction code SM59 to create an RFC destination.
  • Configure RFC Destination: Enter the connection details for MuleSoft, such as the host, port, and system ID.

Step 3: Develop RFC Function Modules in SAP (ABAP Developer can help create an RFC in SAP)

  • Create RFC Function Modules: Develop function modules in SAP ECC that will be called by MuleSoft. These modules should expose the necessary functionality (e.g., fetching employee data)
  • Test Function Modules: Ensure the function modules work correctly within SAP.

Step 4: Set Up MuleSoft Integration

  • Create a New Project: Open Anypoint Studio and create a new Mule project.
  • Add SAP Connector: Use the SAP connector in MuleSoft to connect to the SAP system. Configure the connector with the RFC destination details.
  • Develop Integration Flow: Create a flow in MuleSoft to call the RFC function modules. Use DataWeave for data transformation if needed.

Step 5: Test and Deploy

  • Test Integration: Test the integration flow to ensure it correctly calls the RFC function modules and processes the data.
  • Deploy Integration: Deploy the integration flow to a MuleSoft runtime environment.

Additional Resources

MuleSoft Documentation: Refer to the SAP Connector User Guide for detailed instructions and examples.

MuleSoft Community: Join the MuleSoft community forums for support and additional resources.

 

While MuleSoft is a powerful integration platform, there are some limitations to consider when using it for SAP HR integrations:

  • Steep Learning Curve
  • Customization Challenges
  • Initial Investment & Ongoing Maintenance
  • Dependence on Third-Party Connectors
  • Limited Out-of-the-Box Support

Despite these limitations, MuleSoft remains a powerful tool for integrating SAP HR systems with other applications and systems. Proper planning, expertise, and ongoing maintenance can help overcome these challenges and achieve successful integrations.

 

Conclusion:

Integrating SAP HR systems with Mulesoft offers a powerful solution to streamline and enhance human resource management processes. By leveraging Mulesoft's robust API capabilities, organizations can seamlessly connect SAP On Premise HCM & SuccessFactors Employee Central with other HR systems, ensuring real-time data synchronization, improved operational efficiency, and a unified view of employee information. This integration not only supports better decision-making but also enhances the overall employee experience, driving strategic growth and competitive advantage.

Happy Exploring & Learning!!

entry.title: Re: OData authorization Error in Pulic Cloud
entry.content:
Some months ago I faced a similar issue when deploying an app that used an API.

I found SAP Note "3424818 - Restricted usage of S/4 released API services as found on the SAP BusinessAccelerator Hub in custom UIs". This note mentions the following "It is not supported to use S/4 released API services as found on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for custom UI applications. Using these API services for custom UIs is prevented during the deployment of the UI application to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system"

I'm a bit surprised you didn't get an error while you deployed the app. I thought that was not permitted.

entry.title: Lean Warehouse Overview and Implementation Benefits
entry.content:
Hi

Today I am going to share this blog for Lean WM Implementation benefits.

Lean Warehouse Management in SAP

The lean warehouse solution focuses on simplicity and efficiency. It is tailored for businesses with less complex warehousing needs, often characterized by lower volumes and fewer product variations. Lean warehouses streamline operations by minimizing unnecessary processes and focusing on the core functionalities necessary for effective warehouse management. When you implement the Warehouse Management System (WMS), you manage goods movements and stock changes in the warehouse at storage bin level. However, if you implement Lean WM.  inventory management takes place solely at storage location level. The system does not update the stock data at storage bin level using the quants. You use Lean WM solely for processing goods receipts and goods issues. Using Lean WM, you process the warehouse movements in basically the same way as if using the Warehouse Management System: you work with deliveries, and you create transfer orders for these deliveries. These transfer orders serve as pick lists

Functionalities and Features

Lean warehouses offer a pared-down set of functionalities that cover essential warehouse operations, including:

- Basic inbound and outbound processing

- Simple inventory management

- Standard RF transactions

- Basic reporting and analytics

The use of transfer orders in Lean WM provides the following advantages:

You can reprint transfer orders at any time.

You can split transfer orders and thus distribute the workload better among the staff in your warehouse.

You can pass on the transfer order target data and the names of those who process transfer orders to the HR application component, where this data can be used to calculate incentive wage.

If you do no wish to update stocks in your fixed bin warehouse at storage bin level, Lean WM provides you with the option of using the advantages of warehouse management through transfer orders in the same way as warehouse management using the WMS. Since Lean WM does not update stocks at storage bin level, you can display the stock quantities solely in Inventory Management (IM) and not with the stock overview function in WM. Also, you cannot process possible stock differences that occur in the warehouse using Lean WM, only using Inventory Management (MM-IM)

 

 

Implementation and Maintenance

SAP recommends that you implement Lean WM if you wish to pick deliveries in a warehouse that you are not managing using WMS. Lean warehouse implementations are quicker and less resource-intensive. The streamlined nature of the solution means fewer customizations and simpler configuration processes. Maintenance is also more straightforward, with fewer components to manage and update, resulting in lower ongoing costs.

Prerequisites

You can only implement Lean WM in a fixed bin warehouse; random storage is not possible. Before you can implement Lean WM, you need to make the following configuration changes in Customizing:

  1. Assign a (newly defined) storage location to a warehouse number.
  2. Set up at least two storage types:
  • a picking storage type as the source storage type
  • A shipping area as the destination storage type for deliverie

Configuration

In order to be able to use Lean WM in your warehouse, you must first execute the following settings in Customizing for Shipping.

IMG- Logistic Execution-Shipping- Picking- Lean WM

Map following activity as Highlighted descriptions are coming under IMG- Logistic Execution-Shipping- Picking- Lean WM

Defining control parameters and number ranges for the warehouse number.

Note-We recommend that you always use warehouse number 100 as a warehouse to copy when you implement Lean WM.

Defining storage type for Lean WM.

In the SAP standard version, different examples of Lean WM storage types are preset in warehouse number 100.

Defining the picking area

In the material master, a picking area must be assigned to the material in the view Plant data / Storage 1. In the SAP standard version, two picking areas are preset.

Note-Lean WM is a prerequisite for implementation of picking areas.

Defining transfer types

In the SAP standard version, transfer type A (outbound delivery) is preset for warehouse number 100 (Lean WM). 

Defining movement types

The movement type for Lean WM supplies the information that is required for inbound and outbound deliveries.

  • Destination storage type that appears on the printout of the transfer order.
  • Control indicator for processing, confirming, and issuing transfer orders (single print or combined print).
  • Movement types 101 , 601 , and 255 are preset for Lean WM. SAP recommends keeping these movement types.

 

Defining difference indicator

the difference indicator, you can classify stock differences. This indicator is used in Lean WM for evaluation purposes only.

Defining print control

 

Synchronizing movement types between Inventory Management (MM-IM) and the Warehouse Management System (Lean WM)

Note-In the SAP standard version, all the relevant movement types are preset. SAP recommends working with the standard version.

Defining degree of activation for Warehouse Management (selection between Lean WM and full WM)

Note-Control Plant / Storage Location / Warehouse Number Assignment

 

Scalability and Flexibility

 

While lean warehouses provide sufficient flexibility for many small to medium-sized businesses, they might face limitations when scaling up to meet the demands of a rapidly growing enterprise or more complex logistical requirements

Cost Considerations

Lean warehouse solutions are more cost-effective, with lower upfront costs and reduced long-term financial commitments. This makes them an attractive option for businesses operating on tighter budgets or those seeking a quicker return on investment

Conclusion

lean warehouse solutions depend largely on the specific needs and goals of your business. if simplicity, cost-effectiveness, and quicker implementation are your priorities, a lean warehouse approach may be more suitable.

 

Reference https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/b2dee5e83e2446149294f9860a7c08f0/ceadbd53d34ab64ce10000000a174cb4.html
